summary.intent: Address two Codex review findings on PR #731 in the fogodev/ars-ui repo: fix Dioxus form status-region detection failing for wrapped components, and correct stale spec parity text in fieldset docs.
summary.outcome: Both Codex findings were addressed: a new `has_status_region` (or equivalent) prop was added to the Dioxus form component for explicit wrapper opt-in, a regression test was added and verified green, fieldset spec parity sentences were corrected, and the fix was committed and pushed to PR #731.

repo: Dioxus SSR VNode scanning cannot traverse arbitrary wrapper components, so detecting child StatusRegion by VNode name fails for wrapped cases — explicit root-level props are the correct escape hatch.
The repo uses `cargo xclippy`, `cargo xfmt`, and `cargo xtask spec validate` as the standard pre-push lint/format/spec validation pipeline.
CI matrix includes Leptos and Dioxus wasm coverage lanes that are among the slowest jobs; the aggregate Coverage check only passes after both complete.
The repo uses Codex as an automated PR reviewer via `@codex review` comments on GitHub issues.

code.finding: StatusRegion child detection was VNode-name-based, which cannot see through wrapper components. Fixed by adding an explicit prop to signal status-region ownership.
Regression test added for the wrapped-component status-region case; test was confirmed red before the fix and green after.
Parity sentence was missing 'Content' — stale copy that diverged from actual component behavior.
Same stale parity sentence as Leptos fieldset spec; both were fixed together.

workflow: Polling CI and Codex state by watching PR check runs and review thread counts via GitHub GraphQL API is an effective async monitoring pattern.
Triggering a fresh Codex review after a fix commit is done via `@codex review` comment on the PR issue, not a dedicated API endpoint.
Running the focused test (`cargo test -p ars-dioxus --test form`) in a red state before patching the implementation confirms the test genuinely covers the gap.
Committing Codex-suggested fixes with `Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>` is the expected attribution convention in this repo.

friction: Extended polling loop (~15+ minutes) waiting for CI matrix jobs (especially Leptos/Dioxus wasm coverage) and Codex review to complete.
GitHub check runs did not immediately attach to the new commit after push, requiring an additional wait cycle.
`cargo xclippy` ran through split i18n feature checks after the main workspace pass, adding unexpected latency before the commit could proceed.
open_items: Unit Tests and Release Verification CI jobs were still pending when Codex's verdict arrived; their final status was not confirmed in the transcript.
The VNode-name-based StatusRegion detection for the direct (non-wrapped) case was left in place alongside the new explicit prop — the two detection paths may warrant consolidation later.
The Dioxus form spec was updated for the wrapper behavior but the broader wrapper escape-hatch pattern may not be documented at the architecture/guide level yet.
